title: hibbertia cistiflora subsp. quadristaminea
intro: Hibbertia cistiflora subsp. quadristaminea, also known as the Guinea flower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae, native to eastern Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Hibbertia cistiflora subsp. quadristaminea include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various pathogens. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or infusion, and it can also be found in various commercial products such as creams and ointments, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
